title: Chocolate fudge cake
subtitle: Two-layer chocolate buttermilk cake with fudge frosting
excerpt: A deep, moist two-layer chocolate cake made with buttermilk and cocoa, filled and covered with a thick chocolate fudge frosting. The buttermilk reacts with the bicarbonate of soda for a tender, open crumb. Refrigerate after assembling; bring to room temperature before serving.
sourceNotes: Chocolate layer cakes with butter-and-sugar frostings are documented in American domestic cookbooks from the late nineteenth century, many available through the Internet Archive. The combination of cocoa, buttermilk, and bicarbonate of soda as a leavening system is documented in American household baking manuals from the 1880s onward, where the acid-base reaction is noted as producing a more tender crumb than baking powder alone. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: chocolate cakes with butter-based icing are documented in the chapter on iced cakes.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted sponge layers. Defrost at room temperature and frost before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The frosted cake keeps refrigerated for 3 days. Bring to room temperature for 30 minutes before serving — cold frosting is firm and flavourless.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf. If baking both layers simultaneously, swap shelves at 15 minutes for even baking

glossaryTerms.definition: A chocolate frosting made by melting dark chocolate with butter and cream, then beating in icing sugar until thick and spreadable. Unlike a soft ganache, fudge frosting is worked to a stiffer consistency that holds its shape when spread. It sets slightly firm on the surface of the cake but stays soft when cut, and carries a stronger chocolate flavour than a buttercream.

content.text: This is a straightforward chocolate layer cake: two moist sponges made with cocoa and buttermilk, sandwiched and covered with a thick chocolate frosting. The buttermilk keeps the crumb tender and reacts with the bicarbonate of soda for a more open texture than a cream-based sponge. The frosting is the main event — make it thick and generous.

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Butter and line two round tins. Whisk together the {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. In a separate bowl, whisk the {{eggs}}, melted {{unsalted-butter}} for the sponge, {{buttermilk}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and whisk until smooth. Divide evenly between the two tins. Bake for 28–30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean and the sponges are pulling away from the edges. Cool in the tins for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack.

Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} and {{double-cream}} together in a small saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove and leave to cool for 15 minutes until thickened. Beat in the {{unsalted-butter}} for the frosting and the sifted {{icing-sugar}} until you have a thick, glossy frosting. This is the
. If it is too soft to spread, refrigerate for 15 minutes.

Place one sponge on a plate. Spread one third of the frosting over the top. Place the second sponge on top and spread the remaining frosting over the top and sides. Refrigerate for 30 minutes to set before serving. Bring to room temperature for 30 minutes before eating.
